title: activity — queued jobs
description: Four queued jobs. `BackfillActivityTenantIdFromJsonbJob` is a one-shot install migration reading `tenant_id` out of the vendor `properties` JSONB blob for existing rows. `PurgeExpiredActivitiesJob` runs nightly and respects per-tenant retention policies. `ArchiveActivitiesToColdStorageJob` runs weekly sending >90d rows to S3 cold storage. `ReconcileActivityBatchesJob` runs nightly closing orphan batches from crashed requests.

jobs.description: One-shot install migration. Reads `tenant_id` out of the vendor's `properties` JSONB blob and populates the newly-added `tenant_id` column. Batched (10000 rows per iteration) to avoid table locks; runs against rows WHERE tenant_id IS NULL AND properties->>'tenant_id' IS NOT NULL. Fires once per tenant on Wave 6 upgrade; verifies via a companion `activity:backfill-tenant-id --verify` command that no orphans remain.

Nightly retention sweep. For each tenant: read the effective retention_days (per-log_name policy override OR config default 90d), delete activity rows WHERE created_at < now() - retention_days AND tenant_id = tenant. Respects `activity_retention_extended` entitlement — tenants without it are capped at 90d regardless of override. Fires ActivityPurgeExecuted per tenant per log_name. Batched to 5000 rows per iteration.

Weekly cold-storage rotation. For each tenant with retention > 90d: read activity rows in the 90d..retention_days window that haven't yet been archived, serialise them to Parquet, upload to the tenant's cold-storage bucket (config.activity.cold.bucket_pattern), and mark them archived. Rows past retention_days are deleted by PurgeExpiredActivitiesJob instead — this job only moves rows out of the hot table for tenants with extended retention.

Nightly orphan-batch sweep. Finds `batch_uuid` values where ActivityBatchStarted fired but ActivityBatchCompleted never did (request crashed OR was rolled back). For each orphan: emit ActivityBatchCompleted with `activitiesWritten=0` + `orphan=true` metadata so downstream analytics can distinguish orphans from real batches. Deletes the batch_uuid from the tracking cache. Runs against tracking cache older than 24 hours — legitimately long-running requests (bulk imports) don't get swept.

activity.description: Default queue for activity-adjacent jobs (subscribers, formatters). High volume; sampled at 100%.

retention.description: Batched retention sweeps (purge, archive, reconcile). Runs off-hours per schedule.json.

installer.description: One-shot migration jobs (BackfillActivityTenantIdFromJsonbJob). Fires once per Wave-6 upgrade; empty at steady state.
